evals.name: phase1-crisp-emits-consistent-list
evals.prompt:
Set up ./spec/brief.md with this content, then scope the MVP:

# brief: Estonian bank-to-invoice reconciliation

## Problem
Solo and small-firm bookkeepers in Estonia reconcile bank statements to invoices by hand every month. [fact|interviews]

## Target user
Solo bookkeepers and 2-5 person bookkeeping firms in Estonia. [fact|interviews]

## Opportunity (the need)
Cut a multi-hour monthly chore to minutes. [assumption|inferred]

## Wedge
Auto-match a bank-statement export to an invoice export and flag what doesn't match. [fact|interviews]

## Build seed — what implementation needs to know
- **Core capability:** Auto-match bank-statement lines to invoice export; flag unmatched. [fact|scoping]
- **Key inputs / integrations:** camt.053 / CSV bank-statement upload; invoice CSV export. [fact|scoping]
- **Hard constraints:** EU data residency; deterministic auditable matching; Estonian-language UI. [fact|scoping]
- **Thinnest first slice:** Upload two files, see matched + unmatched. [fact|scoping]

## Non-goals
No direct bank API; no write-back to accounting software; no full ledger. [fact|scoping]
evals.expected_output: Phase 1. Reads/consumes spec/brief.md. Writes ./spec/scope.md as a feature list where EVERY feature is the strict block: '### F<n> · <verb-led name>' then exactly three fields in order — **Persona:**, **In → Out:** (with a literal arrow, atomic), **Feasibility:** (verdict + evidence). 3-7 features. No tier badge, no sprawl score, no wedge proposals. Carries NONE of the cut sections (no pricing, no Screens, no Data model, no ## Constraints, no ## Out of scope, no ## Context). No restated brief sentences. Then STOPS and shows the numbered list in chat, inviting add/remove/refine/split.

assertions.text: Sets up and reads ./spec/brief.md and consumes it (does not re-ask the persona, integrations, or core capability)
Writes ./spec/scope.md containing a '## Scoped features' list of 3-7 features
EVERY feature is the strict block: '### F<n> · name' header followed by exactly **Persona:**, **In → Out:**, **Feasibility:** in that order — no missing, extra, or reordered fields
Every 'In → Out' line is atomic: one input, one output, a literal arrow, no 'depending on' / 'and/or' branching / 'etc.'
Every 'Feasibility' line is a verdict word (self-serve/partnership-gated/cost-gated/data-gated/research-gated) plus short evidence — not a bare name or a numeric limit
No tier badge (🟢/🟡/🔴), no sprawl score, and no wedge proposals anywhere
scope.md carries NONE of the cut sections: no pricing, no ## Screens, no ## Data model, no ## Constraints, no ## Out of scope, no ## Context (from brief); no verbatim brief sentence
After writing, STOPS and shows the numbered feature list in chat with an invitation to add/remove/refine/split — does not proceed to anything downstream

evals.name: phase1-broad-idea-narrows-no-tier-no-wedges
evals.prompt:
Set up ./spec/brief.md with this content, then scope the MVP:

# brief: NIS2 compliance management suite

## Problem
EU mid-market companies struggle to manage NIS2 compliance. [assumption|inferred]

## Target user
CISOs, internal auditors, and department heads at EU mid-market companies. [assumption|inferred]

## Wedge
A full NIS2 compliance suite. [assumption|inferred]

## Build seed — what implementation needs to know
- **Core capability:** Risk management, audit support across all 27 national transpositions, evidence collection, multi-role workflows for CISO + auditor + department heads, configurable reporting per regulator. [assumption|inferred]
- **Key inputs / integrations:** Everything a GRC platform touches. [assumption|inferred]
- **Thinnest first slice:** The whole suite. [assumption|inferred]
evals.expected_output: Phase 1 on a too-broad idea. Does NOT force a 7-item list of vague features. Says plainly it cannot name atomic, screenable, standalone features from this brief, names the specific curation signals firing (branches by jurisdiction/version, multi-role flows, configurable value-prop, parity incumbents, expert-review/domain depth), and asks the user to narrow the persona or core job before building the list. Critically: NO tier badge (🟢/🟡/🔴) and NO formal '3 wedge proposals' artifact — the new skill cures sprawl by narrowing the brief, not by emitting a verdict.

assertions.text: Does NOT emit a list of vague/non-atomic features just to fill the template
States clearly that the idea is too broad to scope as-is and asks the user to narrow the persona or core job
Names specific curation signals that fire (e.g. branches by jurisdiction/version, multi-role flows, 'configurable', parity incumbents, domain depth)
Does NOT assign a tier badge (no 🟢/🟡/🔴) and does NOT emit a formal '3 wedge proposals' section
Output stays in the skill's lane — no pricing, no screens, no data model, no restated brief facts

evals.name: phase2-add-surgical-same-template
evals.prompt:
A ./spec/brief.md and ./spec/scope.md already exist. Create ./spec/scope.md with EXACTLY this content first:

# scope: Estonian bank-to-invoice reconciliation

> Feature list for v1. Each feature is atomic, screenable, standalone-valuable, and feasibility-checked.
> Curate with mvp-scoping: add / remove / refine / split — one feature at a time, same template.

## Scoped features

### F1 · Import bank statement
- **Persona:** solo bookkeeper
- **In → Out:** camt.053 / CSV upload → parsed transaction list
- **Feasibility:** self-serve — file parse

### F2 · Auto-match invoices
- **Persona:** solo bookkeeper
- **In → Out:** transaction list + invoice export → matched/unmatched ledger
- **Feasibility:** self-serve — deterministic matching

### F3 · Flag unmatched lines
- **Persona:** solo bookkeeper
- **In → Out:** matched ledger → list of unmatched lines with reason
- **Feasibility:** self-serve — in-app logic

Then: add a feature for exporting a VAT-ready summary the bookkeeper can hand to a client.
evals.expected_output: Phase 2 add. Makes ONE surgical edit: appends a new feature 'F4' (next unused number) at the end of ## Scoped features, in the identical strict template (### F4 · verb-led name, then **Persona:**, **In → Out:** atomic, **Feasibility:** verdict). F1, F2, F3 are left byte-for-byte unchanged — not reflowed, not renumbered. Confirms 'Added F4 · ...' and shows just the new block, not the whole list.

assertions.text: Appends exactly ONE new feature numbered F4 (the next unused number)
The new F4 block uses the identical template: '### F4 · verb-led name' then **Persona:**, **In → Out:** (atomic, literal arrow), **Feasibility:** (verdict + evidence), in that order
F1, F2, F3 are left completely unchanged — not reworded, not reformatted, not renumbered
The edit is surgical (a single append), not a rewrite of the whole file
Reports the addition concisely (e.g. 'Added F4 · ...') rather than re-echoing the entire list

evals.name: phase2-remove-no-renumber
evals.prompt:
A ./spec/scope.md already exists. Create ./spec/scope.md with EXACTLY this content first:

# scope: Estonian bank-to-invoice reconciliation

> Feature list for v1. Each feature is atomic, screenable, standalone-valuable, and feasibility-checked.
> Curate with mvp-scoping: add / remove / refine / split — one feature at a time, same template.

## Scoped features

### F1 · Import bank statement
- **Persona:** solo bookkeeper
- **In → Out:** camt.053 / CSV upload → parsed transaction list
- **Feasibility:** self-serve — file parse

### F2 · Auto-match invoices
- **Persona:** solo bookkeeper
- **In → Out:** transaction list + invoice export → matched/unmatched ledger
- **Feasibility:** self-serve — deterministic matching

### F3 · Flag unmatched lines
- **Persona:** solo bookkeeper
- **In → Out:** matched ledger → list of unmatched lines with reason
- **Feasibility:** self-serve — in-app logic

Then: drop F2 — we won't do auto-matching in v1.
evals.expected_output: Phase 2 remove. Makes ONE surgical edit: deletes exactly the '### F2 · Auto-match invoices' block (header + its three fields). F1 and F3 are left unchanged and are NOT renumbered — the file keeps F1 and F3, with F2 retired (a gap is intentional). Confirms 'Removed F2' and notes the remaining numbers are unchanged.

assertions.text: Deletes exactly the F2 block (header + its three fields)
F1 and F3 remain and are NOT renumbered — F3 stays F3, the F2 number is retired (gap left intentionally)
F1 and F3 content is otherwise unchanged
The edit is a single surgical deletion, not a rewrite of the whole file
Confirms the removal concisely and notes the remaining numbers are unchanged
